title: Theme toggle and larger website text
summary: Added a persistent dark/light theme toggle and increased the dashboard's baseline text sizing for easier reading across controls, cards, logs, forms, and watcher task output.
cause: A reported website change asked for dark and light mode and larger text.
fixed: The top toolbar now includes a theme toggle that persists in localStorage.
Light theme color variables are defined for idle, running, and error states.
Core dashboard text, buttons, inputs, logs, task cards, and changelog entries now render larger.
comments: How it should improve: the dashboard is easier to read and can switch between dark and light modes without waiting for a branch or PR.

title: Bots should start exploring at 0,0
summary: Automated repair completed and validation passed for Bots should start exploring at 0,0.
cause: A site bug report or user report queued this repair.
fixed:
The bots don’t start at 0,0

Page: http://bigheados.com:3000/
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (iPhone; CPU iPhone OS 26_5_0 like Mac OS X) AppleWebKit/605.1.15 (KHTML, like Gecko) CriOS/148.0.7778.166 Mobile/15E148 Safari/604.1
Public IPv4: 24.114.59.124
Observed IPv4: 24.114.59.124

opencode --prompt timed
comments: How it went: automated opencode ran non-interactively on autofix/bots-should-start-exploring-at-0-0-c2e7da658e and validation passed.
How it should improve: add or keep regression coverage for this signature so the watcher catches repeat issues sooner.

title: Direct-push website repair workflow
summary: Changed automated website bug and feature repairs to validate in a detached scratch worktree, push directly to main, and redeploy the website without creating new feature branches or PRs.
cause: Feature requests should be processed directly on main and deployed immediately instead of creating new git branches for the user to merge later.
fixed: Bug watcher tasks now target main in direct-push mode instead of generating autofix branches.
Validated fixes push with git push origin HEAD:main and then trigger the existing website rebuild/restart path.
The watcher status UI now labels the mode as direct push and describes tasks as targeting main rather than waiting on branch auto-merge.
comments: How it should improve: future feature requests and bug reports should land on main and deploy without a branch/PR handoff.

title: Feedback: Color code tasks
summary: Automated repair completed and validation passed for Feedback: Color code tasks.
cause: Automated validation detected Feedback: Color code tasks.
fixed:
User feedback requested improvements for report 1779344113509-c5d3b2ad.

Report title: Color code tasks
Original details:
Make tasks color coded and animated to make it easier to see what it’s doing, also add a longer summary of all changes made in the task and log file and agent file available to e
comments: How it went: automated opencode ran non-interactively on autofix/batch-20260521-0d306b45 and validation passed.
How it should improve: add or keep regression coverage for this signature so the watcher catches repeat issues sooner.

title: Bug watcher task visibility and auto-fix recovery
summary: Hardened automated repair worktrees against Git safe-directory failures and made watcher tasks easier to read, color-coded, animated, and exportable from the site.
cause: A reported auto-fix task failed after Git rejected the watcher worktree as dubious ownership, and user feedback said the task text was too small and not visibly color-coded.
fixed: Auto-fix and auto-merge worktrees are added to Git safe.directory before validation, commit, push, or merge operations.
Watcher task cards now use larger readable text, severity-colored borders, animated queued/running states, and expanded details.
Generated agent prompts and session logs are exposed as downloadable artifacts when available.
features: Changelog entries can link to exported watcher logs and generated agent prompt files.
comments: How it should improve: failed watcher tasks caused by safe-directory ownership checks can be retried without hitting the same Git blocker, and users can inspect richer task context directly from the website.

title: Automated bug watcher and site tools
summary: Added queued bug reports, auto-approval, lifecycle comments, IPv4 consent capture, background validation, automated opencode repair branches, PR comments, changelog viewing, and world zip import.
cause: The control panel needed a zero-interaction way to capture site issues, treat warnings as small errors, and keep fixes traceable.
features: Report Bugs modal queues automated repair tasks and only asks once for IPv4 collection consent
Users can add public comments to bug reports after submission
Checker queues related improvements from user feedback comments automatically
After every automated fix, the checker rebuilds the control image and restarts the site container
Bug watcher stores reports and sessions in logs/bug-watcher
Reports are auto-approved, updated while processing, and finished with public comments, cause, summary, branch, session log, and PR URL
Each unrelated error gets an autofix branch while related errors share a signature
Multiple queued requests can be batched into one branch for combined processing
Watcher panel shows task-in-progress, processing queue, pending merge, and merged fix lists with elapsed time and ETA
Queued tasks can be removed with a confirmation dialog
Failed automated tasks retry up to five times before being marked failed
Failed queue attempts wait thirty minutes before the next retry
Bug watcher task processing uses opencode --prompt from each task worktree
Failed watcher tasks can be selected individually or with select-all and requeued for processing
Watcher task cards show cancel/remove controls for queued or running work and retry controls for failed work
Queued retry tasks can be retried immediately, and generated autofix branches are sanitized before worktree creation
Non-zero opencode --prompt exits now count as failed attempts instead of silently passing validation afterward
Automated site restarts are handed to a helper container so the control panel does not leave itself stopped
Process All Tasks Now clears retry waits, requeues failed tasks, and immediately starts the watcher queue
Timed-out opencode --prompt runs are force-stopped and followed by validation so the queue cannot hang forever
Completed fixes auto-merge to main after five minutes unless rejected
Changelog modal shows dated entries colored by severity
World zip import auto-detects classic and dimensions world roots
comments: How it went: the control panel gained a public request lifecycle so submitted reports no longer disappear after queuing.

How it should improve: future watcher runs should add regression checks for repeated signatures, keep comments synced to pull requests, use user feedback comments as follow-up requirements, and always refresh the running site after a fix.
